questions.question: State the three conditions required for a valid solution to the critical section problem. Explain each condition with an example.
keyPoints: Mutual exclusion: Only one process in CS
Progress: Decision among waiting processes, cannot postpone indefinitely
Bounded waiting: Limit on how many times others enter before a request is granted

questions.question: Prove that Peterson's solution satisfies all three critical section requirements.
keyPoints: Mutual exclusion: If both in CS, flag[0]=flag[1]=true, but turn can only be 0 or 1
Progress: If Pj not interested (flag[j]=false), Pi enters immediately
Bounded waiting: After Pi sets turn=j, at most one entry by Pj

questions.question: Explain the Test-and-Set instruction. Write code to implement mutual exclusion using Test-and-Set.
keyPoints: TestAndSet atomically reads and sets to true
Spin while lock is held: while(TestAndSet(&lock));
